issues.name: combine-dunning-workers
issues.description: Combine DunningSweepWorker and DunningAttemptWorker into a single worker
steps: Create single DunningWorker that runs periodically (e.g., every few hours)
Query all past_due subscriptions where next_retry_at is in the past
Loop through each subscription and attempt rebill via NMI
Update database after each attempt for idempotency (in case of crash)
On success: renew subscription, create payment record, reset retry counter
On failure: increment retry count, schedule next retry, or mark failed if max retries
Remove old DunningSweepWorker and DunningAttemptWorker
Update River job registration

issues.name: test-dunning-worker
issues.description: Test combined DunningWorker processes failed payments
steps: Create multiple past_due subscriptions with next_retry_at in past
Mock NMI client to return success/failure responses
Run DunningWorker
Verify subscriptions renewed on success, or failed on decline
Verify payment records created for successful rebills
Verify subscriptions not yet due are not processed

issues.name: stripe-like-api-structs
issues.description: Create Stripe-like response envelopes (Object wrappers) with our pagination system
steps: Create `pkg/api/response.go` defining standard response structs.
Define `ListResponse` struct with `object: list`, `data`, `total_items`, `page`, `page_size`, `total_pages` fields (NOT Stripe's has_more - we prefer explicit pagination).
Define wrappers for core resources: `ProductObject`, `PriceObject`, `SubscriptionObject`.
Define `PaymentIntentObject` and `NextActionObject` for handling redirects.
Update existing handlers to use these wrappers for JSON responses (starting with `GetProducts`).
Verify tests still pass or update them to expect new JSON structure.

issues.name: stripe-like-api-products
issues.description: Refactor Product and Price endpoints to match Stripe patterns
steps: Rename route `GET /v1/subscriptions/products` to `GET /v1/products`.
Add `GET /v1/prices` endpoint to list individual prices.
Update `GetProducts` handler to return `ListResponse` of `ProductObject`.
Update `GetProducts` handler to map internal models to new API structs.
Update tests to use new routes and validate new response format.

issues.name: stripe-like-api-subscriptions
issues.description: Refactor Subscription creation endpoints to cleaner RESTful patterns
steps: Change `POST /v1/subscriptions/process/:processor` to `POST /v1/subscriptions/:provider` (e.g., /mobius, /ccbill, /solana).
Register explicit routes for each provider rather than using a dynamic :provider param to avoid collision with :id.
Move `POST /v1/subscriptions/ccbill/flexform-url` to `POST /v1/subscriptions/ccbill` (same endpoint, just generates FlexForm URL).
Simplify webhooks: `POST /v1/webhooks/:provider` instead of `/v1/subscriptions/webhook/:processor/:provider` (e.g., /webhooks/mobius, /webhooks/ccbill).
Update tests to use new endpoint paths.

issues.name: stripe-like-api-rest
issues.description: Consolidate user-scoped endpoints under /v1/me/ and standardize RESTful patterns
steps: CURRENT: Only `GET /v1/me/status` exists under /v1/me/
Move `GET /v1/subscriptions/active` + `GET /v1/subscriptions/history` → `GET /v1/me/subscriptions` with query params (?status=active, ?status=all)
Move `GET /v1/subscriptions/purchases` → `GET /v1/me/payments`
Move `POST /v1/subscriptions/cancel` → `POST /v1/me/subscriptions/cancel`
Move `GET /v1/payment-methods` → `GET /v1/me/payment-methods` (also POST, PUT, DELETE)
Move `GET /v1/wallet/solana/*` → `GET /v1/me/wallets/*` (all wallet endpoints)
Move `GET /v1/notifications/*` → `GET /v1/me/notifications/*` (all notification endpoints)
Refactor Solana payment endpoints: `POST /v1/solana/generate` → `POST /v1/payment-intents`, `POST /v1/solana/submit` → `POST /v1/payment-intents/:id/confirm`, etc.
Update all corresponding tests.

issues.name: time-mocking
issues.description: Add time-mocking capability for testing time-dependent logic
steps: Research options: benbjohnson/clock, jonboulle/clockwork, or custom solution
Create Clock interface that wraps time.Now() and can be injected
Update services/handlers to use Clock interface instead of time.Now() directly
Add SetTime() or Advance() methods to test clock for controlling time in tests
Test subscription expiry: create sub expiring Nov 30, advance to Nov 30, verify expiry logic
Test dunning/retry logic with time advancement

issues.name: cleanup-worker
issues.description: Add cleanup worker for expired temporary data
steps: Identify all temporary tables/records that need cleanup (wallet challenges, pending payments, etc.)
Create River job for periodic cleanup (e.g., CleanupExpiredDataArgs)
Implement cleanup logic: delete expired wallet challenges, pending Solana payments, stale sessions
Add configurable retention periods for each data type
Schedule worker to run periodically (e.g., every hour or daily)
Add metrics/logging for cleanup operations
Write tests to verify expired data is cleaned up correctly

issues.name: river-workers-in-tests
issues.description: Start River workers in test suite for async job testing
steps: Initialize River client in TestContainerSuite setup
Register all worker types (WebhookProcess, DunningAttempt, DunningSweep, etc.)
Start River workers in background during test setup
Add helper to wait for job completion in tests
Test webhook processing flow end-to-end (webhook -> job -> subscription created)
Test dunning sweep enqueues dunning attempt jobs
Gracefully stop workers in test cleanup

issues.name: nmi-demo-integration
issues.description: Add NMI demo account to test suite for real API integration tests
steps: Configure testcontainer suite with NMI demo credentials (security key: 6457Thfj624V5r7WUwc5v6a68Zsd6YEm)
Add NMI config to TestContainerSuite with test_mode enabled
Create test for Subscribe endpoint with real NMI API calls
Use test card number 4111111111111111 with expiry 10/25
Test successful payment creates subscription and payment record
Test declined payment (amount < $1.00) returns appropriate error
Test dunning worker rebill with real NMI API

issues.name: amounts-in-cents
issues.description: Store amounts in cents (integers) instead of floats
steps: Change Price.Amount from float64 to int64 (cents)
Change Payment.Amount from float64 to int64 (cents)
Create migration to convert existing amounts (multiply by 100)
Update all services to work with cents internally
Update API responses to return amount in cents (Stripe convention)
Update API requests to accept amount in cents
Add helper functions for cents <-> dollars conversion if needed for display
Update seed data and tests to use cent amounts

issues.name: processor-jsonb-field
issues.description: Consolidate processor config on Price model into single JSONB 'processors' field
steps: Add 'processors' JSONB field to Price model (map[string]string)
Create migration to add processors column
Migrate existing nmi_plan_id/nmi_provider/ccbill_price_id data to new processors field
Update PriceService to read/write from new processors field
Update Subscribe handler to check processors map for processor availability
Update Solana payment service to check processors map for 'solana' key
Update CCBill handler to check processors map for 'ccbill' key
Update webhook handlers to lookup prices by processor key
Remove old nmi_plan_id, nmi_provider, ccbill_price_id columns
Update seed data helpers to use new processors format
Update tests to verify processor-specific price filtering

issues.name: test-cancel-auth-boundaries
issues.description: Add tests for cancel subscription authorization boundaries
steps: Test that User A cannot cancel User B's subscription (should get 'no active subscription' error)
Test that admin can cancel any user's subscription by subscription ID
Test that admin can cancel any user's subscription by user ID
Verify database status changes to 'cancelled' after successful cancel
Verify entitlements are revoked after cancel

issues.name: ccbill-upgrade-url
issues.description: Add CCBill upgrade URL generation endpoint
steps: Create POST /v1/subscriptions/ccbill/upgrade-url endpoint
Accept target_price_id and validate user has active CCBill subscription
Generate CCBill upgrade FlexForm URL with user's existing subscription info
Return upgrade URL for frontend to redirect user
Document CCBill Admin setup requirements (Package ID, Package Upgrade Allowed)
Write tests for upgrade URL generation

issues.name: ccbill-upgrade-entitlements
issues.description: Update entitlements on subscription upgrade
steps: In handleUpgradeSuccess, get new product's EntitlementsSpec after ActivateWithPrice
Revoke old entitlements that aren't in the new product's spec
Grant new entitlements from the upgraded product
Use existing entitlement granting logic (from lifecycle_service or admin_subscription_service)
Write tests for entitlement updates on tier upgrade
Handle edge case: same entitlements in both tiers (extend end dates)

issues.name: test-subscription-renewal-webhook
issues.description: Test that renewal webhook extends subscription period
steps: Create active subscription with period ending soon
Simulate renewal webhook from processor (NMI or CCBill)
Verify CurrentPeriodEndsAt moved forward by billing cycle days
Verify payment record created for the renewal

issues.name: test-failed-payment-webhook
issues.description: Test that failed payment webhook sets subscription to past_due
steps: Create active subscription
Simulate failed payment webhook from processor
Verify subscription status becomes past_due
Verify next_retry_at is scheduled for dunning

issues.name: test-cancel-access-at-period-end
issues.description: Test user cancellation keeps access until period end, then revokes
steps: Create active subscription with entitlements
User cancels (RevokeAccess: false)
Verify user still has entitlements immediately after cancel
Advance clock to period end
Trigger period-end processing
Verify entitlements are revoked after period ends

issues.name: test-admin-revoke-access
issues.description: Test admin revocation removes access immediately
steps: Create active subscription with entitlements
Admin revokes access (RevokeAccess: true)
Verify entitlements are revoked immediately
Verify subscription status is cancelled

issues.name: test-dunning-retry-schedule
issues.description: Test dunning worker respects retry schedule with clock advancement
steps: Create past_due subscription with next_retry_at in future
Run dunning worker - verify no attempt made (not yet due)
Advance clock past next_retry_at
Run dunning worker - verify rebill attempt made
On failure, verify next_retry_at incremented correctly (+1 day, +3 days, etc.)
Verify retry_count incremented

issues.name: test-dunning-max-retries
issues.description: Test subscription fails after max dunning attempts
steps: Create past_due subscription near max retries
Mock NMI to return decline
Advance clock and run dunning worker for final attempt
Verify subscription status becomes failed
Verify entitlements are revoked

issues.name: test-dunning-success-reactivates
issues.description: Test successful dunning reactivates subscription
steps: Create past_due subscription
Mock NMI to return success
Advance clock and run dunning worker
Verify subscription status becomes active
Verify new period dates set correctly
Verify payment record created

issues.name: test-entitlement-expiry
issues.description: Test time-limited entitlements expire correctly
steps: Grant 15-day entitlement to user
Verify entitlement is active immediately
Advance clock 14 days - verify still active
Advance clock 1 more day (15 total) - verify no longer active

issues.name: test-entitlement-stacking
issues.description: Test entitlement stacking extends expiry
steps: Grant 15-day entitlement to user
Grant another 15-day entitlement of same type
Verify expires_at is 30 days from original grant
Advance clock 20 days - verify still active
Advance clock 10 more days - verify no longer active

issues.name: test-indefinite-entitlement
issues.description: Test indefinite entitlements never expire
steps: Grant entitlement with null duration (indefinite)
Verify entitlement is active
Advance clock 1 year
Verify entitlement is still active

issues.name: test-payment-intent-expiry
issues.description: Test expired Solana payment intents are rejected
steps: Create Solana payment intent with 10-minute expiry
Verify intent is valid immediately
Advance clock 15 minutes
Attempt to submit payment - verify rejected as expired

issues.name: test-wallet-challenge-expiry
issues.description: Test wallet verification challenges expire
steps: Create wallet challenge with expiry
Advance clock past expiry
Attempt to verify challenge - verify rejected as expired

issues.name: fix-test-cancel-access-at-period-end
issues.description: Fix TestCancelAccessAtPeriodEnd to properly test period-end revocation with mock clock
steps: Current test uses indefinite entitlement (EndAt: nil) which never expires regardless of time
Need to either: (a) create a period-end worker that revokes entitlements, or (b) create entitlement with EndAt set to period end
If using period-end worker: inject mock clock into worker, run worker after advancing clock past period end
If setting EndAt: set entitlement EndAt = subscription period end, then verify IsEntitled returns false after advancing clock
Ensure test actually verifies entitlements are revoked at period end, not just that subscription is cancelled

issues.name: fix-test-dunning-retry-schedule
issues.description: Fix TestDunningRetrySchedule to actually run the dunning worker with mock clock
steps: Current test only checks timestamps, doesn't run the actual dunning worker
Inject mock clock into DunningWorker
Run DunningWorker when clock is before next_retry_at - verify NO rebill attempt made
Advance clock past next_retry_at
Run DunningWorker again - verify rebill attempt IS made
This requires DunningWorker to use injected clock for time comparisons

issues.name: fix-test-dunning-max-retries
issues.description: Fix TestDunningMaxRetriesFailsSubscription to use mock clock and run dunning worker
steps: Current test never advances mock clock and calls FailMembership directly instead of running worker
Inject mock clock into DunningWorker
Create subscription with retry_count at max-1
Run DunningWorker (with mocked NMI returning decline)
Verify subscription is cancelled and entitlements revoked
This tests the actual dunning flow, not just the FailMembership service method

issues.name: fix-test-dunning-success-reactivates
issues.description: Fix TestDunningSuccessReactivates to use mock clock and run dunning worker
steps: Current test advances clock but doesn't use it - calls RenewMembership directly
Inject mock clock into DunningWorker
Create past_due subscription with next_retry_at in the past (relative to mock clock)
Run DunningWorker (with mocked NMI returning success)
Verify subscription is reactivated with correct period dates
This tests the actual dunning flow, not just the RenewMembership service method

issues.name: fix-test-payment-intent-expiry
issues.description: Fix TestPaymentIntentExpiry to use mock clock instead of time.Now()
steps: Current test uses time.Now() directly - Solana handlers don't use mock clock
Add Clock field to SolanaPaymentIntentService or relevant handlers
Update expiry check to use injected clock instead of time.Now()
Create payment intent with ExpiresAt = mockClock.Now() + 10 minutes
Verify intent is valid at mockClock.Now()
Advance mock clock 15 minutes
Attempt to check/submit - verify rejected as expired (using mock clock time)

issues.name: fix-test-wallet-challenge-expiry
issues.description: Fix TestWalletChallengeExpiry to use mock clock instead of time.Now()
steps: Current test uses time.Now() directly - SolanaVerificationService uses time.Now()
Add Clock field to SolanaVerificationService
Update expiry check in VerifyChallenge to use s.Clock.Now() instead of time.Now()
Create challenge with ExpiresAt = mockClock.Now() + 10 minutes
Verify challenge is valid at mockClock.Now()
Advance mock clock past ExpiresAt
Attempt to verify - verify rejected as expired (using mock clock time)

issues.name: unify-admin-auth
issues.description: Remove separate admin port and API key, use JWT with admin role claims instead
steps: CURRENT: Admin API runs on separate port with API key authentication
PROBLEM: API key is a shared secret - if stolen, attacker has full admin access with no audit trail
Add admin role/claims check to existing JWT validation middleware (e.g., middleware.AdminRequired)
Move admin routes from separate adminHandler to publicHandler, protected by AdminRequired middleware
Remove BillingAPIKey from config
Remove separate admin server/port setup
Update admin route paths if needed (currently /v1/subscriptions/:id/extend, /v1/users/:user_id/entitlements, etc.)
Update api-server to pass through JWT instead of using API key
BENEFIT: Each admin request tied to user identity, full audit trail, no shared secrets

issues.name: admin-rate-limiting
issues.description: Add rate limiting to admin endpoints to limit blast radius of compromised JWT
steps: PROBLEM: If admin JWT is leaked, attacker could cancel/refund thousands of users before detection
Implement per-admin-user rate limiting using Redis (keyed by admin user ID from JWT)
Define rate limits for destructive operations: e.g., 5 cancellations/minute, 10/hour, 50/day
Define rate limits for bulk operations: e.g., 3 extend operations/minute
On rate limit exceeded: lock out admin for extended period (e.g., 1 hour) and alert
Add alerting/notification when rate limits are approached (e.g., 80% threshold)
Create admin rate limit middleware that wraps destructive endpoints
Allow super-admin or manual override to unlock rate-limited admin if legitimate
Log all rate limit events for security audit
BENEFIT: Limits blast radius - attacker can only affect ~5-10 users before getting locked out

issues.name: unix-epoch-timestamps
issues.description: Standardize all API response timestamps to unix epochs (like Stripe)
steps: Audit all response structs in internal/handlers/responses.go for time.Time fields
Audit pkg/api/response.go - already uses int64 unix epochs (good)
Convert time.Time fields to int64 unix epochs in: SubmitPaymentResponse, PaymentStatusResponse, GeneratePaymentResponse, SolanaPaymentQRResponse, etc.
Use existing api.ToUnix() and api.ToUnixPtr() helpers for conversions
Update any handlers that return time.Time directly to use unix epochs
Ensure webhook event timestamps also use unix epochs
Update tests to expect int64 timestamps instead of time.Time strings
Document in API that all timestamps are unix epoch seconds (like Stripe)

issues.name: prefixed-resource-ids
issues.description: Add Stripe-like prefixes to resource IDs in API layer (prod_xxx, price_xxx, sub_xxx)
steps: NOTE: Prefixes should only exist in the API layer, NOT in the database. Database keeps clean UUIDs for indexing/joins.
Create pkg/api/id.go with helper functions: FormatProductID(uuid) -> 'prod_xxx', FormatPriceID(uuid) -> 'price_xxx', FormatSubscriptionID(uuid) -> 'sub_xxx', FormatPaymentID(uuid) -> 'pay_xxx'
Create ParseProductID(string) -> uuid that strips 'prod_' prefix and validates
Create ParsePriceID, ParseSubscriptionID, ParsePaymentID similarly
Update response conversion functions (ProductToAPI, PriceToAPI, etc.) to use FormatXxxID()
Update request handlers to use ParseXxxID() when accepting IDs from clients
Return clear error if ID prefix doesn't match expected resource type (e.g., 'prod_xxx' passed to subscription endpoint)
Update tests to use prefixed IDs in requests and expect them in responses
BENEFIT: Easier debugging, self-documenting IDs, matches Stripe pattern

issues.name: structured-error-responses
issues.description: Standardize error responses to match Stripe's error format
steps: CURRENT: Errors return simple {message: 'error text'}
TARGET: Errors return {error: {type, code, message, param}} like Stripe
Create pkg/api/error.go with ErrorResponse and ErrorDetails structs
Define error types: 'invalid_request_error', 'authentication_error', 'authorization_error', 'api_error', 'card_error', 'idempotency_error'
Define error codes: 'missing_param', 'invalid_param', 'resource_not_found', 'authentication_required', 'insufficient_permissions', etc.
Update Request.ErrorJSON() in internal/handlers/request.go to accept structured error info
Create helper functions: InvalidParamError(param, message), NotFoundError(resource), AuthRequiredError(), etc.
Update handlers to use structured error helpers instead of plain strings
Update tests to expect structured error format
BENEFIT: Clients can programmatically handle errors by type/code, better UX
